description: Implement the core kernel engine modules: graph executor (reads graph.yaml and executes workflow nodes), state manager (reads/writes state.yaml and memory files), knowledge store (CRUD for rules/skills/patterns with indexing), evolution engine (self-modification of graph.yaml and prompts), skill composer (combines multiple skills), and the reflector logic. These are the Python modules that make the kernel functional.
steps: Implement kernel/graph_executor.py: A class GraphExecutor that (1) loads graph.yaml, (2) resolves the current node from state, (3) returns the node's prompt file path and metadata, (4) determines next node based on conditions/transitions, (5) supports adding/removing/reordering nodes (for evolution). Methods: load_graph(), get_current_node(), get_prompt_for_node(node_id), advance(result), get_available_transitions(), validate_graph(). The graph.yaml format: {nodes: [{id, prompt_file, transitions: [{to, condition}], max_retries}]}.

Implement memory/state_manager.py: A class StateManager that (1) loads/saves state.yaml, (2) tracks iteration count, (3) manages current_goal.md, plan.md, progress.yaml, (4) appends to decisions.jsonl and reflections.jsonl, (5) provides state snapshots. Methods: load_state(), save_state(), get_state(), set_current_node(node_id), increment_iteration(), record_decision(decision_dict), record_reflection(reflection_dict), update_progress(tasks_total, tasks_done), is_complete(), reset().

Implement knowledge/store.py: A class KnowledgeStore that (1) reads/writes rules (manual and learned), (2) reads/writes skills with composability metadata, (3) reads/writes patterns, (4) maintains _index.yaml files for each category, (5) supports search/filter by tags/name. Methods: add_rule(rule_dict, learned=False), get_rules(filter=None), add_skill(skill_dict), get_skill(name), list_skills(tags=None), compose_skills(skill_names) -> combined prompt, add_pattern(pattern_dict), get_patterns(filter=None), rebuild_index(category). Index format: {items: [{name, path, tags, description, created_at}]}.

Implement knowledge/skill_composer.py: A class SkillComposer that (1) takes a list of skill names, (2) loads each skill's SKILL.md, (3) resolves dependencies between skills, (4) combines them into a single coherent prompt/context, (5) handles conflicts (e.g., two skills with contradictory rules). Methods: compose(skill_names) -> str, validate_composition(skill_names) -> list of issues, get_dependency_graph(skill_name) -> dict, resolve_order(skill_names) -> ordered list.

Implement kernel/evolution/engine.py: A class EvolutionEngine that (1) reads the current graph.yaml, (2) accepts proposed changes (add node, remove node, reorder, modify prompt), (3) validates changes against constitution.md (cannot modify protected files), (4) applies changes, (5) logs to history.jsonl. Methods: propose_change(change_dict), validate_change(change_dict) -> (bool, reason), apply_change(change_dict), get_history(), rollback(change_id). Protected paths: ['kernel/BOOT.md', 'kernel/constitution.md', 'runner.py']. Change dict format: {type: 'add_node'|'remove_node'|'reorder'|'modify_prompt'|'add_skill'|'add_rule', details: {...}, timestamp, reason}.

Implement kernel/reflector.py: A class Reflector that (1) analyzes iteration results (what worked, what failed), (2) proposes evolution changes, (3) extracts learned rules from experience, (4) writes reflections to memory/reflections.jsonl. Methods: analyze_iteration(iteration_data) -> reflection_dict, propose_evolution(reflections) -> list of change proposals, extract_rules(reflections) -> list of rule dicts, summarize_progress() -> str.

Update runner.py to use these modules: The main loop should instantiate GraphExecutor, StateManager, KnowledgeStore, and wire them together. The loop: (1) state = StateManager.load_state(), (2) node = GraphExecutor.get_current_node(), (3) prompt = GraphExecutor.get_prompt_for_node(node), (4) context = KnowledgeStore.get_relevant_context(node), (5) output prepared context (in dry-run mode, print it; in normal mode, write to a file for AI consumption), (6) StateManager.increment_iteration(), (7) check stop conditions, (8) advance to next node.
Ensure all modules have proper __init__.py exports and can be imported cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: GraphExecutor can load a graph.yaml, return nodes, advance through transitions
StateManager can load/save state, track iterations, record decisions and reflections
KnowledgeStore can add/get rules and skills, rebuild indexes, compose skills
SkillComposer can take multiple skill names and produce a combined context string
EvolutionEngine can propose, validate, apply, and rollback changes to graph.yaml
EvolutionEngine rejects changes to protected files (BOOT.md, constitution.md, runner.py)
Reflector can analyze iteration data and produce reflection dicts and evolution proposals
runner.py main() executes a dry-run loop without errors: python3.12 runner.py --goal 'test' --max-iterations 1 --dry-run
verification: python3.12 -c 'from kernel.graph_executor import GraphExecutor; g = GraphExecutor("kernel/graph.yaml"); print(g.get_current_node())'
python3.12 -c 'from memory.state_manager import StateManager; s = StateManager("kernel/state.yaml", "memory/"); print(s.get_state())'
python3.12 -c 'from knowledge.store import KnowledgeStore; k = KnowledgeStore("knowledge/"); print(k.list_skills())'
findings: All 152 tests pass with 93.2% coverage (exceeding the 90% threshold). All verification commands pass: runner.py dry-run works, all modules importable, GraphExecutor reads graph.yaml, StateManager manages state, KnowledgeStore lists skills. The test_placeholders.py file was removed since placeholder methods no longer exist. The SkillComposer constructor now takes a KnowledgeStore instance instead of a skills_dir path. The EvolutionEngine constructor takes (kernel_dir, graph_executor) instead of just kernel_root. StateManager takes (state_path, memory_dir) strings instead of a single kernel_root Path.
